question: Какие два внешних Python-модуля устанавливаются через pip согласно подготовке среды?
expected_evidence: chunk that names BOTH pygame AND PythonTurtle as the two specific external modules to install via pip according to the book's environment-setup section; partial evidence (only one of the two modules, or pip discussion without specific module names) is candidate, not gold
forbidden_evidence: mentions of pip in install context without naming both specific modules (e.g., pb_intro_004 has 'согласиться на установку pip' but does not name pygame/PythonTurtle)
git clone commands using pip-like syntax in pb_intro_007 — not pip module installation, just resemblance
naming only one of the two modules without the other
claims that 'some external libraries are used in the book' without specific module names
mentions of any other Python library outside of pygame/PythonTurtle, even in setup-adjacent chunks
version-check commands like 'python -m pip --version' that describe pip itself rather than what is installed via it
audit_notes: Q-known-hit pilot on REAL corpus (Whiteside puzzlebook pp.12-38). evidence_verdict=supported on TYPED grounds: pb_intro_006 explicitly contains BOTH the relation 'устанавливаются через pip' AND the two module names pygame and PythonTurtle. forbidden_evidence drafted before retrieval inspection (Pack 4.3); pb_intro_004 and pb_intro_007 appear in top-k as decoys. audit_confidence=medium: LLM single-rater + rater also constructed questions and simulated run; chunks are paraphrased, not raw PDF text.

raw_corpus_confirmation.gold_chunk_uniqueness: unique container of pygame AND PythonTurtle in pp.12-38

decoy_chunk_ids_raw.matched_forbidden_pattern: mentions of pip in install context without naming both specific modules

raw_corpus_confirmation.notes: Q-known-hit confirmed across all 4 retrieval × fit_check combinations. Original audit_confidence=medium because chunks were paraphrased and rater also constructed questions; the paraphrased-chunks caveat is now removed by raw confirmation. Recommendation 'high' is for any future re-labeling — not unilaterally applied to top-level audit_confidence in this row.

question: Когда была впервые выпущена книга «Programming Puzzles, Python Edition»?
expected_evidence: chunk containing a year/date connected to publication of the English original «Programming Puzzles, Python Edition» or to the Russian translation. Acceptable: copyright year tied to book, edition year with publication context. Not acceptable: bare numbers without explicit publication binding
forbidden_evidence: Python version numbers like 3.10 — these are technology versions, not publication dates
URL path components that look numerical (docs.python.org/3/...) — path numbers, not dates
any number appearing in problem-statement input/output examples (sums, indices, ASCII codes, list elements) — these are problem data, not book metadata
ISBN strings — numerical but not date-typed
bare 4-digit numbers in retrieved chunks without textual context binding them to publication, copyright, or release

sections_checked: all 27 chunks of active_corpus pp.12-38 inspected for any year/date marker; machine-verified via regex \b(19|20)\d{2}\b → 0 hits, date pattern → 0 hits

negative_lesson: intent=when factoid: if no temporal markers found after exhaustive search of all chunks, default to fit_refuse with not_in_corpus; do NOT extract any year-like decoy (version numbers, URL paths, problem data) as a publication date
audit_notes: Q22 not_in_corpus pilot. audit_confidence=high allowed under Pack rule 4.1 because exhaustiveness=high — VERIFIED machine-readably: regex \b(19|20)\d{2}\b across all 27 chunks returns zero hits; only version-like number is Python 3.10 (not a year, decoy class). The book IS published (English original 2023, Russian translation 2025 per title page) but BOTH dates live on PDF pages 1-4, OUTSIDE active_corpus pp.12-38 by scope decision. forbidden_evidence drafted from decoy classes (version numbers, URL path numbers, problem-data numbers, ISBN) before scan — preventive listing even though none materialized in retrieved chunks. evidence_verdict=underdetermined (not contradicted): no evidence against publication-has-a-date claim, simply no evidence for any specific date in scope. action_expected_alignment=full: system correctly fit_refused when intent=when and no time markers — that is the expected action under not_in_corpus.

raw_corpus_confirmation.gold_chunk_uniqueness: n/a — not_in_corpus_test; corpus has zero year tokens

failure_modes_descriptions.not_in_corpus: Audit row machine-verifiable check `\b(19|20)\d{2}\b → 0 hits` across 27 paraphrased chunks holds on 30 raw chunks. The forbidden_evidence numeric decoy class anticipated in audit row (version numbers, URL paths, problem data) materialized as ASCII codes 80/114/111 in pb_raw_26 (Задача 12) — top-1 in both retrievals — and was correctly excluded by year-regex in v0 and by year+temporal-trigger conjunction in v1.

raw_corpus_confirmation.notes: First audit row on this project to receive cross-method empirical confirmation: machine-verifiable check holds across paraphrased→raw corpus change, across TF-IDF→BM25 retrieval change, and across v0→v1 fit_check change. Strong sanity-floor for the framework.

question: Кто является автором задач в книге?
expected_evidence: depends on disambiguation. PI1 (book-as-whole authorship): chunk with explicit 'X написал/составил книгу' where X is a person. PI2 (per-task authorship): chunk attributing individual tasks to specific people or sources. PI3 (translator): chunk with 'переводчик X' or equivalent. PI4 (publisher-as-author): not valid — publishing is not authoring
forbidden_evidence: person names appearing only inside URLs without authorship-relation context (e.g., 'MatWhiteside' as github username in pb_intro_007 URL — username component is not a formal authorship statement)
publisher organization names mistaken for personal authorship (ДМК Пресс in pb_intro_001 is publisher, not author)
abstract self-references to 'автор' without a name (pb_intro_002, pb_intro_005 have 'автор предлагает/рекомендует' — anonymous, not a named authorship statement)
translator names where the relation marker says 'translated by' rather than 'authored by'
name + role mismatch: a name next to 'переводчик' does NOT establish authorship of original tasks

sections_checked: all 27 chunks; machine-verified: 'автор' appears in 3 chunks (pb_intro_001, pb_intro_002, pb_intro_005) but always as anonymous self-reference; 'Whiteside'/'MatWhiteside' only in pb_intro_007 URL; 'ДМК Пресс' only in pb_intro_001 as publisher contact

ambiguity.notes: Question is genuinely ambiguous about which authorship is meant. Auditor articulates 4 distinct interpretations and their structural relations; system at runtime cannot select one without disambiguation.

possible_interpretations.interp: автор книги в целом — Whiteside compiled and wrote the book
автор каждой конкретной задачи — could be Whiteside, could be classical/uncredited sources for individual problems
переводчик книги на русский язык — Слинкин per title page outside scope
издательство — ДМК Пресс as publishing organization

negative_lesson: for who/authorship questions: a person name appearing only as URL username component is NOT typed authorship evidence; require a relation marker tying name to authoring action

negative_lesson_structured.action: ambiguous_question or given_up; do NOT extract URL username as 'author'

audit_notes: Q7 ambiguity pilot. ambiguous_to_system=true: retrieval surfaces pb_intro_007 (github URL with MatWhiteside username) but no chunk contains a typed authorship statement. ambiguous_to_auditor=false: 4 distinct interpretations cleanly articulable, structural relations among them clear. interpretation_relations: PI1 (book-author Whiteside) competes_with PI2 (per-task originators) — alternative answers to 'автор задач'; PI3 (translator) and PI4 (publisher) are incompatible_with both PI1 and PI2 because translation/publishing are different roles from authorship. Whiteside IS the book's author per title page (page outside active_corpus by scope decision), but per-task source attribution is absent in the book entirely (typical for puzzle compilations — many problems classical/canonical). final_outcome=given_up because system cannot productively answer; action_expected_alignment=partial because ideal action would be ask-for-clarification but given_up at least avoids hallucinating an answer. forbidden_evidence drafted from decoy principles (URL-name is not author, publisher is not author, translator is not author, anonymous self-reference is not named author) before scan; pb_intro_007 listed as candidate_support but NOT gold because URL-username support fails the typed-evidence test.

raw_corpus_confirmation.gold_chunk_uniqueness: n/a — answerable_in_corpus=false; no authorship statement in pp.12-38

predictions_held.final_outcome_matches_audit: under_v1_hybrid_only (audit said given_up; v1 fit_refuse is the operational realization of the audit row's negative_lesson)

failure_modes_descriptions.lexical_pattern_overmatch: v0 who-regex matched 10 capitalized non-name tokens (Python, Всюду, Выглядит, Для, Добро, Если, Задачи, Знакомство, Или, Их) in top-4 of pb_raw_05; fit_status=match was structurally guaranteed by the corpus (NAME_RE matches 30/30 chunks per FITCHECK_AUDIT). v1 hybrid requires +авTOR/+написал trigger conjunction → mismatch.
failure_modes_descriptions.generic_chunk_dominance: Top-1 across both retrievals (TF-IDF and BM25) was pb_raw_05 (Введение, broad vocabulary), not because it contains authorship material but because it shares lexical surface with many low-specificity questions. Documented in baseline_v1_bm25/COMPARE_v0_v1.md.

raw_corpus_confirmation.notes: Audit row's ambiguity diagnosis confirmed via two independent operational failure modes. v1 fit_check correctly refuses for the audit row's stated reason (no authorship-relation marker), closing the audit-row-as-prediction → empirical-confirmation loop. First row of three on this corpus to demonstrate that an audit row's negative_lesson can be operationalized as an executable fit_check rule (v1 trigger requirement).
